message:
docs(wills-estate): add GUI-path setup instructions + spouse-usage FAQ + multi-viewport diag spec

Two content changes to the wills & estate planning article and a
companion Playwright diagnostic spec.

================================================================
1) Article: GUI reasoning-tier setup instructions
================================================================

`components/wills-estate-article.tsx` (+22 / -7 in the setup section)

The article previously jumped straight to CLI commands for setting
the top reasoning tier (`claude --model opus --effort max`,
`codex --model gpt-5-codex-max --effort max`), which assumed the
reader was comfortable in a terminal. For the wills-and-estate
audience — explicitly targeted at non-technical readers preparing
their own estate documents — that was a cliff.

New flow: lead with the GUI path for readers on the desktop apps,
keep the CLI path as a secondary option for terminal users.

  - Codex app: click `Select reasoning effort` → pick `Extra High`
  - Claude app: pick `Opus 4.7` as the model, turn on
    `Adaptive Thinking`

The CLI block is preserved verbatim below the GUI block, introduced
as "If you prefer the CLIs, two short commands do the same thing."

Small incidental copy fix in the same region: "my writeup on that
one" → "my post on that one" to match the voice used elsewhere in
the article.

================================================================
2) Article: expanded spouse-usage FAQ
================================================================

`components/wills-estate-article.tsx` (+11 / -2 in the FAQ section)

Previous FAQ answered "Can I share the skill with my spouse?" with
a license-enforcement-first framing (one subscription per user,
watermark, honor system). That missed the actual practical question
most couples are asking, which is "how do I produce my spouse's
documents in the same session."

Rewritten FAQ:
  - Question: "Can I use this for my spouse too?"
  - Answer part 1: yes — run the skill a second time in the same
    session, reusing the working folder, and ask it to generate the
    parallel set of documents for your spouse. Most household facts
    (assets, kids, state of residence) are identical, so the second
    pass is faster than the first.
  - Answer part 2: license clarification — covers your own
    household (you + spouse) under one subscription; does not cover
    sharing/redistribution to anyone else; skill content is
    copyrighted.

Practical answer first, licensing second — the original framing
(watermarking, honor-system deterrence) is dropped because it
addressed a different question (abuse) than what readers were
actually asking.

================================================================
3) Diagnostic Playwright spec: wills-viz multi-viewport screenshots
================================================================

`tests/e2e/_diag-wills-viz.spec.ts` (NEW, 120 lines)

Underscore-prefixed diagnostic spec in the same style as the
existing `_probe-diagram.spec.ts` and `_tmp-diag-debug.spec.ts`
in this directory. Its job is to capture visual snapshots of the
wills visualization sections across six viewports (375, 768, 1024,
1280, 1440, 1920) and two focal targets:

  - "Working folder (Using the skill)" — the section labelled
    `Working folder visualization`
  - "Tier Triage" — the section whose aria-label starts with
    `Wealth tier triage`

Output lands in `/tmp/wills-viz-screenshots/` as
`<target-id>_<viewport-name>.png` so the developer can eyeball the
layout quickly at each breakpoint during iteration. Not gated by
CI and not included in the default playwright run — the `_diag-`
prefix is the repo's established convention for "this is a
dev-only diagnostic, not a regression test" files. It stays in git
so other agents iterating on the wills viz can rerun the same
capture matrix without rebuilding the harness from scratch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

You can run the skill a second time in the same + session, reusing the working folder, and ask it to + generate the parallel set of documents for your + spouse. The skill content is copyrighted and licensed + for personal use the same way a piece of software is, + so please do not post, resell, or hand the files to a + friend. If a friend wants to use it, point them at the + skills site.
